Description: The most permissive option. The rights holder waives all copyright and related rights, placing the work as close to the public domain as possible. No attribution required.
Free to share and adapt for any purpose, including commercially, as long as appropriate credit is given to the original creator.
The current and most widely used open license. Free to share and adapt for any purpose with attribution. Recommended for maximum interoperability.
Free to share and adapt with attribution, but commercial use is not permitted.
Updated version of CC BY-NC. Free to share and adapt with attribution, excluding commercial use.
Designed for intergovernmental organisations. Permits sharing and adaptation with attribution, but prohibits commercial use and requires derivative works to carry the same license.
Free to share and adapt with attribution. Derivative works must be distributed under the same license — a copyleft approach.
Designed specifically for databases. Free to share, modify, and use the database, provided that any adapted version is released under the same license and attribution is given.
Issued by the UK Government. Free to copy, publish, and adapt the data for commercial and non-commercial purposes with attribution.
Data produced by the US federal government and not subject to copyright. Free to use, share, and adapt without restriction or attribution requirement.
If the license you need isn't listed, select Other and provide a name and a link to the relevant documentation.
